verdict_reasoning: A2M is a large secreted protease inhibitor (~720 kDa) that circulates in plasma. While it transiently binds cell-surface receptors and proteoglycans via reversible non-covalent interactions and can be internalized via endocytosis, it lacks a membrane anchor (TM domain, GPI anchor, or lipidation) and is not stably tethered to a TM partner. The protein remains in equilibrium with the soluble pool and would wash away; it is not stably surface-anchored via its own mechanism.
